statement.markdown: Given a doubly ended queue, remove the node at the front of the queue when the queue is not empty, but remove the node at the end of the queue when the queue is empty. Implement the method to handle this edge case.
statement.inputFormat: A doubly ended queue is implemented as a doubly linked list with head and tail pointers.
statement.outputFormat: Remove the front node from the queue.
statement.notes: You must handle the queue edge cases where it is empty.

examples.explanation: Remove the front node from the queue

hints.text: Use a doubly linked list to implement the queue.

editorial.title: Queue Node Removal
editorial.intuition: Implement a doubly linked list to manage the nodes in the queue.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a separate data structure to keep track of the front and rear nodes.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a doubly linked list to remove the front node.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Check the time and space complexity of the solution.
editorial.dryRun: Test the solution with small inputs.
